channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: SP Group Pursues $2.7B Debt at 14% Yield After Unit Downgrade
item.description: India's Shapoorji Pallonji Group plans a $2.7 billion debt raise, securing over half via commitments for dollar and rupee bonds. The group faces a tough funding market, highlighted by a credit downgrade for its unit, Goswami Infratech, to B+ from BB-. Dollar bonds are guided at 14.00%-14.50%, signaling high borrowing costs to refinance liabilities amid massive group debt of about $6 billion.

item.title: NCC Profit Falls 19% on Margin Squeeze; Dividend Proposed
item.description: NCC Ltd reported a 18.8% drop in Q4 FY26 net profit to ₹206 crore, despite a 1.7% revenue increase. Profitability suffered from a narrower EBITDA margin (8.8% vs 9.1%), likely due to rising costs or a less profitable project mix. NCC still recommended a ₹2.20 per share dividend, aiming to reward shareholders amid positive sector outlook.

item.title: India Solar Boom: PM Scheme Sparks Demand, Lifts Companies
item.description: India's Pradhan Mantri Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana is driving rapid growth in the rooftop solar sector, expected to significantly expand the market and create strong demand for key industry players. This government initiative provides generous subsidies, encouraging household adoption and building strong demand for solar manufacturers, installers, and related services. Despite ongoing implementation challenges, the scheme acts as a powerful driver for India's renewable energy goals and a significant economic boost for the solar industry.

item.title: ITC Hotels Buys Kerala Resort, Expands to Target 250 Hotels
item.description: ITC Hotels posted a strong Q4 FY26, with net profit climbing 22.96% to ₹315.89 crore and revenue up 18.2% to ₹1,253.70 crore. They are acquiring The Zuri Kumarakom, Kerala Resort & Spa for ₹205 crore, marking their first owned resort in the state. This acquisition supports an aggressive expansion plan to reach 250 hotels by 2031, following a record 33 hotel signings in FY26. The hospitality sector continues to face rising operational costs due to global events, despite robust domestic demand.

item.title: PVV Infra Plans ₹50 Cr Warrant Issue to Fund Expansion
item.description: PVV Infra is planning a ₹50 crore fundraising through convertible warrants. This move, pending shareholder approval, aims to boost authorized share capital from ₹120 crore to ₹170 crore. The capital will fund future expansion and operations. The company also authorized its Managing Director for National Highways Logistics Management Way Side Amenities (WSA) project documentation and appointed Deepika Sharma as an Independent Director with strong real estate and infrastructure experience.

item.title: India-UK Trade Deal Hits Snag Over UK Steel Quotas
item.description: India and the UK are nearing a Free Trade Agreement, but new British measures on steel imports are creating a major obstacle. The UK plans to cut tariff-free steel quotas by nearly 60% from July 2026. Indian officials say this could slash expected export gains and bring uncertainty, though both nations aim to find a solution to protect the broader trade pact.

item.title: UK Steel Tariffs Threaten India Trade Deal; 'Creative Solution' Needed
item.description: India and the UK are close to finalizing their trade deal, signed on July 24, 2025. But a new UK steel safeguard measure, effective July 1, 2026, threatens to cut tariff-free steel imports by 60% and add a 50% tariff on excess volumes. Both sides are seeking a 'creative solution' to navigate these trade hurdles and ensure the pact proceeds smoothly amid global protectionism.

item.title: Gland Pharma Stock Falls Despite 97% Profit Surge
item.description: Gland Pharma reported a strong Q4 FY26, with net profit surging 96.6% year-on-year to ₹366.6 crore on a 22.3% revenue jump to ₹1,742.7 crore, boosted by its CDMO business and US sales. However, the company's shares fell 1.80% by Friday's close. This drop suggests investor worries about future guidance, industry challenges, or valuation, despite operational gains and a proposed ₹20 per share dividend.

item.title: Indian Banks Slash Loan Write-Offs to Multi-Year Lows as Quality Soars
item.description: Public sector banks in India have achieved multi-year lows in loan write-offs during FY25-26, a trend driven by significantly lower fresh non-performing asset (NPA) formation and enhanced recovery efforts. This improved asset quality, marked by historically low gross and net NPA ratios, has underpinned record aggregate net profits for these institutions. The shift reflects a strategic focus on resolution-led asset management and stronger provisioning, positioning PSBs for sustained profitability despite potential macro-economic shifts.

item.title: India Ramps Up Russian Oil: Moscow Becomes Top Supplier Amid Crisis
item.description: Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov confirmed a major increase in crude oil exports to India, solidifying a key energy partnership that defies global pressures. This surge, driven by India's need for energy security during escalating Middle East tensions and U.S. sanctions waivers, makes Russia India's primary supplier. It highlights New Delhi's assertive 'strategic autonomy' in managing its energy needs and signals a shift in global energy flows.

item.title: Monika Alcobev FY26 Profit Soars 39% on Premium Sales, Valuation Doubts Surface
item.description: Monika Alcobev reported a 22% net profit increase to ₹22.32 crore in Q4 FY26 and a 39% jump in FY26 profit to ₹32.14 crore, with revenue reaching ₹301.16 crore. Growth was fueled by premium tequila, Irish whiskey, and imported wines, alongside expansion into new markets like Kerala. The company highlighted supply chain resilience. Current market data indicates a P/E ratio of approximately 15.95, with a market capitalization around ₹543 crore, prompting some questions about its valuation.

item.title: India Unemployment Hits 5.2%, Masking Deeper Labor Market Strain
item.description: India's official unemployment rate edged up to 5.2% in April, meeting market forecasts. However, this rate masks growing concerns: labor force participation has fallen, and the gap between urban and rural joblessness is widening. Despite strong hiring intentions, external pressures like high energy costs and geopolitical issues cloud the economic outlook, highlighting persistent structural issues.

item.title: Waaree Lands Contract for India's Integrated Green Hydrogen Hub
item.description: Waaree Clean Energy Solutions Private Ltd. (WCES) has won a contract to build an integrated green hydrogen ecosystem at TMEIC Industrial Systems India's facility in Tumkur. This project features hybrid electrolysers and an EV charging unit powered by fuel cells, demonstrating a comprehensive approach to clean energy. The initiative aligns with India's National Green Hydrogen Mission, positioning WCES as a replicable model for the nation's energy transition and bolstering Waaree Energies' market standing.

item.title: Bharat Forge Builds ₹1,500 Cr Defence Hub for Global Exports
item.description: Agneyastra Energetics, a subsidiary of Kalyani Strategic Systems Ltd. (KSSL), has broken ground on a ₹1,500 crore defence manufacturing campus in Andhra Pradesh. This significant private-sector investment aims to establish a globally competitive ecosystem for advanced energetics and ammunition. The project is set to bolster India's indigenous capabilities, reduce import reliance, and position the nation as a potential exporter of critical defence materials, aligning with the 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' initiative.

item.title: Hindustan Copper's Q4 Profit Doubles, Yet Stock Dips on High Valuation
item.description: Hindustan Copper Ltd. reported net profit more than doubled to ₹444.27 crore for Q4 FY26, fueled by a 58% jump in revenue to ₹1,156 crore and improved margins to 54.3%. The company also proposed a dividend of ₹1.86 per share. However, the stock dipped over 4% on results day. This reaction, alongside its P/E ratio around 88, has raised concerns about its valuation compared to rivals Vedanta and NALCO.

item.title: RBI Frees Small NBFCs From Registration, Unlocking Capital
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is creating a new category for certain low-risk Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs), called 'Unregistered Type I NBFCs,' effective July 1, 2026. This exemption applies to companies with assets below Rs 1,000 crore that do not use public funds or have customer interaction. The RBI's move aims to simplify compliance, reduce regulatory burdens, and encourage these firms to deploy more capital into India's equity markets.
